questions: Discord whitelist source: env var `DISCORD_USER_WHITELIST` (comma-separated Discord user IDs)? Or read from a `users` table? (Plan assumes env var.)

Supabase access from Python: do you want a single direct `psycopg`/`psycopg2` connection (cleanest for transactions + advisory locks), or supabase-py's REST client (no multi-statement transactions; would force every atomic op into a Postgres function)? (Plan assumes direct psycopg, with supabase-py only for Storage.)

Mid-turn messages — is it acceptable for the resident loop to handle them as a *follow-up turn* triggered immediately after the current turn completes (instead of injecting them mid-prompt as the spec describes)? The spec's mid-prompt injection requires modifying `run_turn` to support an end-of-turn re-prompt; the follow-up-turn approach satisfies the acceptance criteria with a smaller surface change.

For the Supabase epic lock, do you want row-based (`epic_locks` table, mirrors SQLite, observable, recoverable) or Postgres native `pg_advisory_lock` (session-scoped, auto-released on connection close)? (Plan assumes row-based for parity with SQLite and to keep the recovery scan working.)

Should `images` migrations be added to BOTH the SQLite migrations dir and the Supabase migrations dir this sprint, or only Supabase? (Plan adds to both — the contract tests need it on both stores; if SQLite is now considered deprecated for Sprint 1b+, we can skip the SQLite migration and adjust the contract test to skip image methods on SQLite.)

success_criteria.criterion: DM from a whitelisted user triggers a `run_turn` invocation and a Discord reply within 30s in a mocked end-to-end test (FakeDiscordTransport + FakeModel + SQLiteStore)
DM from a non-whitelisted user produces no Discord reply and writes a `system_logs` row at level=info, category=application, event_type=whitelist_rejected
Every inbound Discord message persists with a unique `discord_message_id` before any LLM call, and a duplicate `discord_message_id` insert is rejected by the DB unique constraint
`run_store_contract(...)` (the unchanged contract suite) passes against both `SQLiteStore` and `SupabaseStore`
5 inbound messages within 8s coalesce into a single turn whose `triggered_by_message_ids` has 5 entries
Killing the runner mid-turn and restarting causes the prior turn to be marked `abandoned` in `bot_turns` and its trigger messages to be re-processed under a fresh `turn_id`
Mocked Groq voice-message pipeline produces a `messages` row with `was_voice_message=true`, `content=<transcription>`, and `audio_storage_url` populated

Mocked Discord image-attachment pipeline downloads to (mock) Supabase Storage and creates an `images` row with `source='user_uploaded'` and an auto-assigned `reference_key` matching `^img_user_upload_\d+$`
`view_image` returns image bytes that the Anthropic adapter encodes as an Anthropic vision content block in the next request
`send_image` (resident) posts to a fake Discord transport with the supplied caption and records an `external_requests` row with provider='discord' that transitions pending→confirmed

Status message lifecycle: turn start → fake transport sees an initial post; each tool call → an edit (subject to 1s debounce); `set_activity` → next edit reflects new 'Currently:' line; turn complete → final edit text starts with '✅ Done.' and contains tool_call count
Status throttling: 20 tool calls within 2s produce at most ~4 Discord edit calls (1s debounce + final), not 20

Mid-turn message: a message arriving while a turn is in flight is persisted immediately, the status message gets a '📥 Received' annotation line, and a follow-up turn is invoked containing the new message in its `triggered_by_message_ids`

External-request reconciliation passes for Anthropic (idempotency-key replay), Discord (post-hoc message lookup → confirmed; not found → orphaned), Groq (deterministic re-issue), Supabase Storage (HEAD-based confirm)
Existing Sprint 1a tests (`tests/test_sqlite_store.py`, `tests/test_run_turn.py`, `tests/test_envelope.py`, `tests/test_ledger.py`, `tests/test_cli.py`, `tests/test_tool_kit.py`) all pass unchanged
`agent_kit/loop.py` changes are minimal — only the optional `triggered_by_message_ids`/`pre_persisted` parameters are added; resident-mode mid-turn handling lives in the new `agent_kit/resident.py`
Each new module is under ~400 lines (`agent_kit/store/supabase.py`, `agent_kit/resident.py`, `agent_kit/transport/discord.py`)

Manual smoke against staging Discord + staging Supabase: whitelisted DM replies within 30s; voice DM transcribes; image attachment lands in Storage with `images` row; mid-turn DM gets the 📥 annotation

assumptions: Discord user whitelist is supplied via env var `DISCORD_USER_WHITELIST` (comma-separated Discord user IDs); changing it requires a restart.
Supabase access uses a direct `psycopg` (psycopg3) connection for store ops (transactions, advisory-style row locks, recovery scans). The supabase-py client is used only for Storage.

Mid-turn messages are handled by spawning a follow-up turn after the current turn completes, not by injecting them mid-prompt into the in-flight turn. This satisfies all listed acceptance criteria with no rewrite of `run_turn`'s control flow.
The Supabase epic lock uses the same `epic_locks` row-based pattern as SQLite (not Postgres `pg_advisory_lock`) so the recovery scan can clean stuck locks across process restarts.
The new `images` table is added to BOTH the SQLite and Supabase migration trees this sprint, so the contract tests stay store-agnostic.

Voice messages are detected via the Discord attachment `voice_message` flag; non-voice audio attachments are out of scope for auto-transcription this sprint (covered by spec adversarial test #14, deferred to a future iteration unless the user wants it included).
Local Supabase dev uses Docker via `supabase start`; tests that need Supabase are skipped when `SUPABASE_TEST_DB_URL` is not set so CI without infra still runs.

`view_image` returns base64-encoded bytes via the result envelope; the Anthropic adapter is responsible for repackaging into a vision block. No new Blob fetch happens inside `agent_kit/model/anthropic.py`.

Reconciliation runs at startup and every 5 minutes via an asyncio task scheduled by `ResidentRunner`. Invocation-mode (CLI) does not run the periodic timer — the spec allows the caller to handle retry, and the boot-time scan is enough.
GitHub reconciliation is left as a no-op log this sprint — the v1 GitHub use is read-only and not exercised in Sprint 1b acceptance criteria.
